column1: # Source: ISO/IEC 27701:2019 Annex A/B normative references
# Note: ISO 27701:2019 extends ISO 27001 — mappings here reflect the PIMS-specific controls and the core management-system clauses.

column9: rationale
Understanding the organization and its context — PIMS extension re-uses 27001 Clause 4.1 verbatim.
Understanding needs and expectations of interested parties, PIMS extends to data subjects as interested party.
Scope of the PIMS — directly layered on 27001 Clause 4.3, PII scope added.
Leadership and commitment — identical ISO 27001 clause with PIMS obligations.
Risks and opportunities — PIMS-specific extension of 6.1.1.
Risk assessment process — 27701 prescribes privacy-specific risk criteria (e.g. DPIA triggers).
Risk treatment — 27701 requires privacy risk treatment register.
Documented information — PIMS demands explicit PII-related documents (records of processing, DPIA, consent evidence).
Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation.
Internal audit — 27701 audits PIMS in the same cadence.
Management review — identical to 27001.
Identify and document purpose — backed by A.5.1 policies.
Identify lawful basis — aligns with A.5.31 legal, statutory, regulatory requirements.
Determine when and how consent is obtained — A.5.1 policy documentation vehicle.
Obtain and record consent — A.8.10 information deletion and A.5.33 records protection adjacent; A.8.10 selected as primary operational link.
Privacy impact assessment — direct correspondence with A.5.34 privacy and PII protection.
Determining and fulfilling obligations to PII principals — A.5.34 is the umbrella control.
Limit collection — A.8.12 data leakage prevention adjacent; A.8.3 information access restriction primary mapping.
Limit processing — classification of information supports purpose-limitation.
PII minimisation — direct A.5.34 privacy objective.
Retention — A.8.10 information deletion is the technical implementation of retention limits.
Disposal — A.8.10 information deletion.
PII transmission controls — A.8.24 cryptography plus A.8.20 networks security.
Identify basis for PII transfer between jurisdictions — A.5.14 information transfer provides baseline.
Countries and international organisations to which PII can be transferred — A.5.14 documentation.
Customer agreement — A.5.20 supplier agreements is the ISO analogue for controller/processor contracts.
Organisations purposes — supplier relationship documentation per A.5.19.
Marketing and advertising use — bound by A.5.34 privacy objectives.
Temporary files — A.8.12 data leakage prevention as operational control.
Return, transfer or disposal of PII — A.8.10 deletion.
